programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Romesh is joined by Kae Tempest, a poet, rapper and playwright from London. They share three songs that have shaped their journey so far, plus a pick from their latest album.
Tony spins super Soul music and celebrates 60 years of Percy Sledge's 'When A Man Loves A Woman', plus 50 years of Shalamar and music from Thelma Jones, Olivia Dean and Bruno Mars.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1983.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1994.
Happy Mondays performing at the Kilburn National in 1992. Featuring Wrote for Luck, Loose Fit, Kinky Afro, Hallelujah and Monkey in the Family.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin has great British artists like All Saints, Sade and The Style Council, with songs that sound perfect in the sun.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Tom Jones, Jose Feliciano and Neil Diamond. This week, the top three comes from 1965.
Phil Dunster chats about his new comedy series with Steve Carrell called Rooster. Plus, some Mercury Rev on vinyl, a wedding call and The Mystery Voice.
Actor Damian Lewis (Wolf Hall, Homeland) joins Romesh live in the studio to chat about his latest project, Orwell: 2+2=5.
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Radio 1 presenters Matt and Mollie pop in to talk about their new podcast, Novel Idea. Plus, Rylan gets an exclusive play of Matt Cardle's new song and sings with him in KaRYoke.
Shaun Keaveny with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Rock hero Sammy Hagar shares his favourite 80s Moments, we celebrate Salt of Salt-N-Pepa's birthday with a Queens Of Rap Mastermix and T'Pau's Carol Decker joins Gary in Hour 2.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Is going into the I'm A Celebrity jungle *basically* like being transported back to simpler 90s times?! Shona is here to discuss that, and her 90s-baby memories.
In her own words, Diana Ross tells the story of her life and career in music, from Motown and the Supremes to playing Billie Holliday and her many solo hits.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: As the clocks go forward, Vernon selects upbeat, uplifting, sing-a-long dance tunes to get you excited for British Summer Time! Plus, there's a Basement Stexx track from Hard Drive.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin invites you to explore the natural world with The Wild About Nature Unwinds Mix, featuring Seal, Tom Petty and Taylor Swift.
The Kane brothers, Pat and Greg, performing live at Edinburgh's Playhouse Theatre in 1989. Featuring Labour Of Love, Ordinary Angel, Violently, Looking For Linda and Remote.
Musician, DJ, the 'M' in M People and Mancuniuan music legend Mike Pickering takes us on a ten-song journey through the songs that have soundtracked his life.
Jason Mohammad marks Palm Sunday with Pause For Thought from Father Brian D'Arcy.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Michael... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy is live from Salford, asking you about the things you've never tried before... Whether it's food, an adventure sport or even a social media.
A celebration of musical theatre hosted by Michelle Visage, packed full of showstoppers and special guest appearances direct from the AO Arena in Manchester.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 70s with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: More great tracks from the 70s. Also on the show, more tracks from Bob's 'Rock Date Diary' plus another 70s Country Classic pick.

Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1978.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
Musical Knights including Elton, Rod & Barry. Paul celebrates The Most Important Meal of the Day! And we have a live track from Marvin Gaye.
Angela invites you to switch off and mellow out with tunes from Jorja Smith, Lemar, Roberta Flack and more.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Escape the grey skies with a playlist made for sunny days, featuring Lenny Kravitz, Everything But The Girl and Spice Girls.
